textEn: Nūn. By the pen and what they inscribe,
You are not, [O Muḥammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.
And indeed, for you is a reward uninterrupted.
textTranslit: Wa inna laka la ajran ghaira mamnoon

textEn: And indeed, you are of a great moral character.
So you will see and they will see.
Which of you is the afflicted [by a devil].

textAr: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ
textEn: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.
textTranslit: Innaa Rabbaka Huwa a'lamu biman dalla 'an sabeelihee wa Huwa a'lamu bilmuhtadeen

textEn: Then do not obey the deniers.
They wish that you would soften [in your position], so they would soften [toward you].
And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer
textTranslit: Wa laa tuti' kulla hallaa fim maheen

textEn: [And] scorner, going about with malicious gossip -
A preventer of good, transgressing and sinful,
Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.
Because he is a possessor of wealth and children,

textAr: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَـٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
textEn: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."
textTranslit: Izaa tutlaa 'alaihi aayaatunaa qaala asaateerul awwaleen

textEn: We will brand him upon the snout.

textAr: إِنَّا بَلَوْنَـٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
textEn: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning
textTranslit: Innaa balawnaahum kamaa balawnaaa As-haabal jannati iz 'aqsamoo la-yasri munnahaa musbiheen

textEn: Without making exception.

textAr: فَطَافَ عَلَيْهَا طَآئِفٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمْ نَآئِمُونَ
textEn: So there came upon it [i.e., the garden] an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep.
textTranslit: Fataafa 'alaihaa taaa'i fum mir rabbika wa hum naaa'imoon

textEn: And it became as though reaped.
And they called one another at morning,

textAr: أَنِ ٱغْدُوا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْثِكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰرِمِينَ
textEn: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit."
So they set out, while lowering their voices,

textAr: أَن لَّا يَدْخُلَنَّهَا ٱلْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكُم مِّسْكِينٌ
textEn: [Saying], "There will surely not enter it today upon you [any] poor person."
And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able.
But when they saw it, they said, "Indeed, we are lost;
textTranslit: Falammaa ra awhaa qaalooo innaa ladaaalloon

textEn: Rather, we have been deprived."

textAr: قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
textEn: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'"
textTranslit: Qaala awsatuhum alam aqul lakum law laa tusabbihoon

textAr: قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ
textEn: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."
textTranslit: Qaaloo subhaana rabbinaaa innaa kunnaa zaalimeen

textEn: Then they approached one another, blaming each other.
They said, "O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors.
textTranslit: Qaaloo yaa wailanaaa innaa kunnaa taagheen

textAr: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رَٰغِبُونَ
textEn: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."
textTranslit: 'Asaa rabbunaaa any yubdilanaa khairam minhaaa innaaa ilaa rabbinaa raaghiboon

textAr: كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ
textEn: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.
textTranslit: Kazaalikal azaab, wa la'azaabul aakhirati akbar; law kaanoo ya'lamoon

textAr: إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
textEn: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure.
Then will We treat the Muslims like the criminals?
What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge?
Or do you have a scripture in which you learn
That indeed for you is whatever you choose?

textAr: أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمَـٰنٌ عَلَيْنَا بَـٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۙ إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَمَا تَحْكُمُونَ
textEn: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?
textTranslit: Am lakum aymaanun 'alainaa baalighatun ilaa yawmil qiyaamati inna lakum lamaa tahkumoon

textEn: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible.

textAr: أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكَآءُ فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ إِن كَانُوا۟ صَـٰدِقِينَ
textEn: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful.
textTranslit: Am lahum shurakaaa'u falyaatoo bishurakaaa 'ihim in kaanoo saadiqeen

textAr: يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
textEn: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but they [i.e., the disbelievers] will not be able,
textTranslit: Yawma yukshafu 'am saaqinw wa yud'awna ilas sujoodi falaa yastatee'oon

textAr: خَـٰشِعَةً أَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ۖ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سَـٰلِمُونَ
textEn: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.
textTranslit: Khaashi'atan absaaruhum tarhaquhum zillatunw wa qad kaanoo yud'awna ilassujoodi wa hum saalimoon

textAr: فَذَرْنِى وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ ۖ سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
textEn: So leave Me, [O Muḥammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies this statement [i.e., the Qur’ān]. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know.
textTranslit: Fazarnee wa many yukazzibu bihaazal hadeesi sanastad rijuhum min haisu laa ya'lamoon

textEn: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm.
textTranslit: Wa umlee lahum; inna kaidee mateen

textAr: أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ
textEn: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?
textTranslit: Am tas'aluhum ajran fahum mim maghramim musqaloon

textEn: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?

textAr: فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلْحُوتِ إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ
textEn: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muḥammad], and be not like the companion of the fish [i.e., Jonah] when he called out while he was distressed.
textTranslit: Fasbir lihkmi rabbika wa laa takun kasaahibil boot; iz naadaa wa huwa makzoom

textAr: لَّوْلَآ أَن تَدَٰرَكَهُۥ نِعْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلْعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ
textEn: If not that a favor [i.e., mercy] from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.
textTranslit: Law laaa an tadaara kahoo ni'matum mir rabbihee lanubiza bil'araaa'i wa huwa mazmoom

textEn: And his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous.

textAr: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجْنُونٌ
textEn: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes [i.e., looks] when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."
textTranslit: Wa iny-yakaadul lazeena kafaroo la-yuzliqoonaka biabsaarihim lammaa saml'uz-Zikra wa yaqooloona innahoo lamajnoon

textEn: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds.
textTranslit: Wa maa huwa illaa zikrul lil'aalameen
